info.title: IncentFit API
info.description:
# Overview

The IncentFit API provides programmatic access to IncentFit's wellness platform. It is organized into the following subsystems:

## REST Data Endpoints
Query activities, fitness data, payments, and activity types. All data endpoints use **POST** with a JSON body containing optional filter, sort, and pagination parameters. Results are automatically scoped to all entities authorized for your API client. Authenticated with an **OAuth2 Bearer token**.

## SCIM User Provisioning
[SCIM 2.0](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7644) endpoints for automated user lifecycle management (list and update users). Data is scoped to all entities linked to the API client. Authenticated with an **OAuth2 Bearer token**.

## Embeddable Widgets
Iframe-embeddable views for activity logging, timeline, devices, rewards, and challenges. Authenticated via a **JWT signed with your RSA private key** — does *not* use OAuth2 Bearer tokens.

## MCP (Model Context Protocol)
JSON-RPC endpoint at `/v2/mcp` for AI tool integrations. Discovery available at `/.well-known/mcp.json`. Authenticated with an **OAuth2 Bearer token**.

get.description: List users with optional SCIM filter and pagination. Returns all users within the API client's entity scope.

200.description: SCIM list response with user resources

post.description: Create a new user. Matches against existing users using the organization's configured matching strategy (email, unique ID, or absolute unique ID). If a matching inactive user is found, they are reactivated.

get.description: Retrieve a single user by ID.

put.description: Full replacement of a user resource (RFC 7644 PUT).

delete.description: Soft-delete a user by deactivating them. Sets eligibility end date to now. Returns 204 No Content.

patch.description: Partial update of a user resource (RFC 7644 PATCH). Accepts add, replace, and remove operations. Commonly used to deactivate users by setting active to false.

get.description:
Ping

Will return the current time in unix timestamp format

200.description: Check if you can connect to the API

post.description: Activities describe specific actions that a user completed
Activity Types provide metadata about an activity, scoped to the requesting entity. Returns global activity types plus those specific to the entity.

offset.description: Number of records to skip. Default: 0.

filter.description: Filter results by field values. Keys are field names (use the same names returned in responses, e.g. `IndividualID`, `StartTime`). ID fields accept SqID-encoded values. Each field contains a `values` array of filter conditions and an optional `operator` (`and`/`or`, default `and`).

operator.description: Logical operator for combining multiple conditions on the same field. Default: and.

value.description: The value to compare against. For ID fields, pass the SqID-encoded string. For `in`/`notIn`, pass an array of values.

dateFrom.description: Start date (alternative to `value` for date fields).

dateTo.description: End date (alternative to `valueTo` for date fields).

sort.description: Sort results by one or more fields. Use the same field names returned in responses (e.g. `StartTime`, `IndividualID`).

columns.description: Column names to search across. Uses the same field names returned in responses.

OrganizationID.description: Optional. SqID-encoded Organization ID to narrow results to a single authorized organization. If omitted, returns activity types across all entities authorized for your API client.

PartnerID.description: Optional. SqID-encoded Partner ID to narrow results to a single authorized partner. If omitted, returns activity types across all entities authorized for your API client.

schema.description: Common request body parameters for REST data endpoints. Results are automatically scoped to the entities authorized for your API client. Use the `filter` parameter to narrow by `OrganizationID`, `UserGroupID`, or any other field.

200.description: Pull raw fitness activities from third party sources

post.description: Payments describe specific payments made to a user

kty.description: The 'kty' (key type) parameter identifies the cryptographic algorithm family used with the key, such as 'RSA' or 'EC'

kid.description: The 'kid' (key ID) parameter is used to match a specific key.

keys.description: An array of JWK Public Keys for this server.

scimType.description: SCIM error type (e.g. uniqueness, invalidValue, invalidFilter)

BrowseRequestBodyWithSince.description: Common request body parameters for REST data endpoints. Results are automatically scoped to the entities authorized for your API client. Use the `filter` parameter to narrow by `OrganizationID`, `UserGroupID`, or any other field.

count.description: Count of results in this page.

offset.description: Offset from start of database result.

limit.description: The max size of the page.

total.description: Total number of records for all for this query.

ActivityID.description: The unique ID of the Activity

IndividualID.description: The ID of the individual who performed the activity

OrganizationID.description: The ID of the organization the individual belongs to

UserGroupID.description: The ID of the user group (incentive plan) the individual belongs to

ActivityTypeID.description: The ID of the Activity Type

IsSelfReported.description: If reported by the user manually

IsAdminReported.description: If reported by an admin manually

IsIncentFitAdminReported.description: If reported by IncentFit staff manually

IsDeviceReported.description: If data recorded originally by a device

IsQRReported.description: If this was reported by someone scanning a QR code

IsSystemReported.description: If reported by the system automatically

IsThirdPartyReported.description: If reported by a third-party integration

OriginalCount.description: Amount of the activity done in the unit reported by the user

OriginalUnit.description: The unit reported by the user

ElevationGain.description: Increase in elevation without subtracting any decrease

FacilityID.description: The ID of the facility attended

FacilityGroupID.description: The ID of the facility group

Title.description: A text description of the activity. Title can depend on which property is the most important.

IsDeleted.description: If the activity has been deleted

count.description: Count of results in this page.

offset.description: Offset from start of database result.

limit.description: The max size of the page.

UnitID.description: The unique ID of the Unit

UnitSingular.description: The name of this unit, singular

UnitPlural.description: The name of this unit, plural

ActivityTypeID.description: The unique ID of the Activity Type

Name.description: The name of this activity type

CanSelfReportActivity.description: Whether or not this type of activity can be self-reported

CanAdminReportActivity.description: Whether or not this type of activity can be admin-reported

CanDeviceReportActivity.description: Whether or not this type of activity can be reported by a fitness tracker

CanGeoLocateActivity.description: Whether or not this type of activity can be verified by geo-located checked-in

CanDocUploadActivity.description: Whether or not this type of activity can be verified by a document upload

CanQRCodeActivity.description: Whether or not this type of activity can be verified by a QR code scan

CanSystemReportActivity.description: Whether or not this type of activity can be reported by the IncentFit system

CanThirdPartyReportActivity.description: Whether or not this type of activity can be verified by a third-party

DocName.description: The name of the document that can be uploaded to verify this type of activity

ImageUrl.description: A URL to an icon image for this activity type

limit.description: The max size of the page.

total.description: Total number of records for all for this query.

OrganizationID.description: The ID of the organization the individual belongs to

UserGroupID.description: The ID of the user group (incentive plan) the individual belongs to

ThirdPartyID.description: Unique ID in source service or auto generated during import

ThirdPartyActivityTypeID.description: The activity type ID reported from the source service.

SourceService.description: The service we received this data from

Offset.description: The timezone offset that was in effect when this activity was recorded

ActivityTypeID.description: The activity type based on the IncentFit activity types lookup table

TrackerID.description: The ID of tracker which recorded this data. Can be looked up in the IncentFit lookup table trackers. Can be a hardware tracker like a watch or Fitbit or an app running on a smartphone or other third party solutions.

AppID.description: The ID of the app which recorded this activity original. Can be looked up in the IncentFit lookup table apps.

ServiceID.description: The ID of the service which recorded this activity originally. Can be looked up in the IncentFit lookup table services. This is not the same as the SourceService field and will only be populated when SourceService is validic

DeviceRecorded.description: If this activity was recorded by a device directly or manually entered.

ElevationChange.description: Change in elevation recorded. Can be negative. In meters.

Bearer.description: Bearer token from the access_token endpoint. Prepend with 'Bearer '
Bearer.name: Authorization

tags.name: Activities
tags.description: REST endpoints for querying activities and activity types. All use POST with optional filter, sort, and pagination parameters. Requires Bearer token.

tags.name: Fitness Data
tags.description: REST endpoints for querying raw fitness device data: exercises, routines, and sleep records. Requires Bearer token.

tags.name: Payments
tags.description: REST endpoint for querying payment records. Requires Bearer token.

tags.name: User Provisioning
tags.description:
SCIM 2.0 ([RFC 7644](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7644)) endpoints for automated user lifecycle management. ## Setup **Base URL:** `https://api.incentfit.com/v2/scim` **Authentication:** OAuth2 Bearer token — obtain via `POST /v2/oauth2/access_token` with `grant_type=client_credentials`, then include as `Authorization: Bearer {token}`. ## Supported Operations
MethodEndpointDescription
GET/v2/scim/UsersList users (with filter and pagination)
POST/v2/scim/UsersCreate user
GET/v2/scim/Users/{id}Get single user
PUT/v2/scim/Users/{id}Replace user (full update)
PATCH/v2/scim/Users/{id}Partial update (e.g. deactivate)
DELETE/v2/scim/Users/{id}Deactivate user (soft delete)
GET/v2/scim/ServiceProviderConfigDiscovery: capabilities
GET/v2/scim/SchemasDiscovery: supported schemas
GET/v2/scim/ResourceTypesDiscovery: resource types
## Entity Scoping SCIM endpoints automatically use all entities (Partners, Organizations, or User Groups) linked to the API client. ## Attribute Mapping Core SCIM attributes (`userName`, `name`, `emails`, `phoneNumbers`, `active`, `externalId`) map directly to IncentFit user fields. Two schema extensions provide additional attributes: **Enterprise User** (`urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:enterprise:2.0:User`): `employeeNumber`, `organization` (read-only), `hireDate` **IncentFit Individual** (`urn:incentfit:params:scim:schemas:1.0:Individual`): `UserGroupID`, `Birthday`, `Gender`, `Tags`, `EligibilityStart`, `EligibilityEnd`, `AbsoluteUniqueID`, `UniqueCompanyID`, `DependentOfEmail`, `DependentOfUniqueID` ## Deactivation Setting `active` to `false` (via PATCH or DELETE) deactivates the user by setting their eligibility end date. Users are never permanently deleted. ## User Matching When creating users (POST), the system matches against existing records using the organization's configured matching strategy (email, unique ID, or absolute unique ID). If a matching inactive user is found, they are reactivated instead of creating a duplicate.
